part.text:
# Gate a Knock workflow with top-level conditions (Node.js)

## Background
[Knock](https://docs.knock.app) workflows can carry a top-level [`conditions`](https://docs.knock.app/mapi-reference/workflows/schemas/workflow) object (a `ConditionGroup` of `all`/`any` rules) that determines whether the workflow runs at all when triggered. When the trigger conditions evaluate to false for a recipient, Knock short-circuits the workflow for that recipient and no channel step is executed.

You are building an internal "admin-only security alert" notification. The same trigger fires for an arbitrary set of users, but only users whose `role` attribute equals `admin` should actually receive the email. You must implement this gate via the workflow's top-level `conditions` (not via per-step conditions or a branch step).

## Requirements
- Create a Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` with a `package.json` and a runnable script `app.js`.
- Use the official [`@knocklabs/mgmt`](https://github.com/knocklabs/knock-mgmt-node) SDK to upsert and activate a workflow in the `development` environment.
- Use the [`@knocklabs/node`](https://github.com/knocklabs/knock-node) SDK to trigger that workflow.
- The workflow must:
  - Have a top-level `conditions` group of type `all` containing a single condition that requires `recipient.role` to be `equal_to` the string `admin`.
  - Contain exactly one channel step targeting the pre-configured `mailtrap` email channel (`channel_key: "mailtrap"`). The step's template must produce an email subject of exactly `Admin alert <run-id>` (substituting the actual run id at upsert time) and a non-empty HTML body that addresses the recipient by `{{ recipient.name }}`.
  - Be marked `active: true` in the `development` environment.
- The trigger call must use **inline recipient identification** to send two recipients in a single `workflows.trigger` request:
  - An admin recipient with `role: "admin"`.
  - A viewer recipient with `role: "viewer"`.
- Append `<run-id>` (from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable) as a suffix to the workflow key, both recipient IDs, the recipient email local-parts, and the email subject so the resource names are safe to use concurrently.
- Write the resulting `workflow_run_id` to the log file in the exact format specified below.

## Implementation Hints
- Read all secrets and shared identifiers from environment variables (`KNOCK_SERVICE_TOKEN`, `KNOCK_API_TOKEN`, `MAILTRAP_DOMAIN`, `GMAIL_USER_NAME`, `ZEALT_RUN_ID`).
- The Management API only accepts writes in the `development` environment; activate the workflow there as well.
- For inline recipients, set both the standard fields (`id`, `name`, `email`) and the custom `role` attribute that the workflow condition references. The email of each recipient must follow Gmail's plus-addressing convention required by the test harness: `<gmail_user_name>+<receiver_id>@gmail.com`.
- The single channel step should use `channel_key: "mailtrap"` (this email channel is already configured in the Knock account). You do not need to configure the channel yourself.
- The Knock workflow key must match the regex `^[a-z0-9_-]+$`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Ensure the real upsert, activate, and trigger actions are executed against the live Knock account (no mocking).
- Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
- The log file must contain a line in the exact format: `Workflow run ID: <workflow_run_id>` where `<workflow_run_id>` is a UUID returned by the Knock trigger API.
- The workflow key in Knock must be `top-level-conditions-${run-id}` where `${run-id}` is the value of `ZEALT_RUN_ID`.
- When fetched via the Management API in the `development` environment, the workflow must:
  - Be `active: true`.
  - Have a top-level `conditions` group whose `all` array contains a single rule with `variable == "recipient.role"`, `operator == "equal_to"`, and `argument == "admin"`.
  - Contain exactly one channel step with `type == "channel"` and `channel_key == "mailtrap"`, whose template `subject` equals `Admin alert ${run-id}`.
- After the trigger executes, the test Gmail inbox must contain exactly **one** new message with subject `Admin alert ${run-id}` addressed to `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+admin-${run-id}@gmail.com`, and **zero** messages with that subject addressed to `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+viewer-${run-id}@gmail.com`.

# Gate a Knock workflow with top-level conditions (Node.js)

## Background
[Knock](https://docs.knock.app) workflows can carry a top-level [`conditions`](https://docs.knock.app/mapi-reference/workflows/schemas/workflow) object (a `ConditionGroup` of `all`/`any` rules) that determines whether the workflow runs at all when triggered. When the trigger conditions evaluate to false for a recipient, Knock short-circuits the workflow for that recipient and no channel step is executed.

You are building an internal "admin-only security alert" notification. The same trigger fires for an arbitrary set of users, but only users whose `role` attribute equals `admin` should actually receive the email. You must implement this gate via the workflow's top-level `conditions` (not via per-step conditions or a branch step).

## Requirements
- Create a Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` with a `package.json` and a runnable script `app.js`.
- Use the official [`@knocklabs/mgmt`](https://github.com/knocklabs/knock-mgmt-node) SDK to upsert and activate a workflow in the `development` environment.
- Use the [`@knocklabs/node`](https://github.com/knocklabs/knock-node) SDK to trigger that workflow.
- The workflow must:
  - Have a top-level `conditions` group of type `all` containing a single condition that requires `recipient.role` to be `equal_to` the string `admin`.
  - Contain exactly one channel step targeting the pre-configured `mailtrap` email channel (`channel_key: "mailtrap"`). The step's template must produce an email subject of exactly `Admin alert <run-id>` (substituting the actual run id at upsert time) and a non-empty HTML body that addresses the recipient by `{{ recipient.name }}`.
  - Be marked `active: true` in the `development` environment.
- The trigger call must use **inline recipient identification** to send two recipients in a single `workflows.trigger` request:
  - An admin recipient with `role: "admin"`.
  - A viewer recipient with `role: "viewer"`.
- Append `<run-id>` (from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable) as a suffix to the workflow key, both recipient IDs, the recipient email local-parts, and the email subject so the resource names are safe to use concurrently.
- Write the resulting `workflow_run_id` to the log file in the exact format specified below.

## Implementation Hints
- Read all secrets and shared identifiers from environment variables (`KNOCK_SERVICE_TOKEN`, `KNOCK_API_TOKEN`, `MAILTRAP_DOMAIN`, `GMAIL_USER_NAME`, `ZEALT_RUN_ID`).
- The Management API only accepts writes in the `development` environment; activate the workflow there as well.
- For inline recipients, set both the standard fields (`id`, `name`, `email`) and the custom `role` attribute that the workflow condition references. The email of each recipient must follow Gmail's plus-addressing convention required by the test harness: `<gmail_user_name>+<receiver_id>@gmail.com`.
- The single channel step should use `channel_key: "mailtrap"` (this email channel is already configured in the Knock account). You do not need to configure the channel yourself.
- The Knock workflow key must match the regex `^[a-z0-9_-]+$`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Ensure the real upsert, activate, and trigger actions are executed against the live Knock account (no mocking).
- Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
- The log file must contain a line in the exact format: `Workflow run ID: <workflow_run_id>` where `<workflow_run_id>` is a UUID returned by the Knock trigger API.
- The workflow key in Knock must be `top-level-conditions-${run-id}` where `${run-id}` is the value of `ZEALT_RUN_ID`.
- When fetched via the Management API in the `development` environment, the workflow must:
  - Be `active: true`.
  - Have a top-level `conditions` group whose `all` array contains a single rule with `variable == "recipient.role"`, `operator == "equal_to"`, and `argument == "admin"`.
  - Contain exactly one channel step with `type == "channel"` and `channel_key == "mailtrap"`, whose template `subject` equals `Admin alert ${run-id}`.
- After the trigger executes, the test Gmail inbox must contain exactly **one** new message with subject `Admin alert ${run-id}` addressed to `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+admin-${run-id}@gmail.com`, and **zero** messages with that subject addressed to `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+viewer-${run-id}@gmail.com`.

The final piece will be to trigger this workflow.

I'm reviewing the Knock Management API documentation to confirm the correct method for activating the workflow in the `development` environment. My current understanding suggests that the `active` attribute within the `workflows.upsert` call is the intended mechanism, rather than a separate `activate` function. I'll proceed with this assumption and verify its implementation.

     *   'workflow_key',

     *     workflow: {
     *       name: 'My Workflow',
     *       steps: [
     *         {
     *           ref: 'channel_1',
     *           template: {
     *             markdown_body: 'Hello **{{ recipient.name }}**',
     *           },
     *           type: 'channel',
     *         },

     *     },
     *   },
     * );
     * ```
     */
    upsert(workflowKey: string, params: WorkflowUpsertParams, options?: RequestOptions): APIPromise<WorkflowUpsertResponse>;

     * Validates a workflow payload without persisting it. Some read-only fields will

output.output:
export interface EmailTemplate {
    /**
     * The [settings](https://docs.knock.app/integrations/email/settings) for the email
     * template. Must be supplied with at least `layout_key`.
     */
    settings: EmailTemplate.Settings;
    /**
     * The subject of the email. Supports Liquid templating with variables like
     * `{{ recipient.name }}`, `{{ actor.name }}`, `{{ vars.app_name }}`,
     * `{{ data.custom_field }}`, and `{{ tenant.name }}`. See the
     * [template variables reference](https://docs.knock.app/designing-workflows/template-editor/variables)
     * for available variables.
     */

input.result:
I have created the Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` and successfully fulfilled all the requirements.

Here is a summary of the implementation:
1. Initialized a Node.js project and installed `@knocklabs/mgmt` and `@knocklabs/node`.
2. Created `app.js` which reads the necessary environment variables (`KNOCK_SERVICE_TOKEN`, `KNOCK_API_TOKEN`, `GMAIL_USER_NAME`, `ZEALT_RUN_ID`).
3. Used the `@knocklabs/mgmt` SDK to upsert a workflow in the `development` environment with `commit: true`. The workflow key is formatted as `top-level-conditions-${run-id}`.
4. Configured the top-level `conditions` group to require `recipient.role` to be `equal_to` `"admin"`.
5. Added a single channel step for `mailtrap` with the specified subject `Admin alert ${run-id}` and an HTML body addressing the recipient by name.
6. Activated the workflow in the `development` environment via the Management API.
7. Triggered the workflow using the `@knocklabs/node` SDK with inline recipient identification for both an `"admin"` and a `"viewer"` user. Both recipients use the plus-addressing convention for the test Gmail inbox.
8. Written the resulting `workflow_run_id` to `/home/user/myproject/output.log` in the exact format required. 

The Knock workflow short-circuits execution for the `"viewer"` recipient, so only the `"admin"` recipient will receive the email in the test inbox.

I have created the Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` and successfully fulfilled all the requirements.

Here is a summary of the implementation:
1. Initialized a Node.js project and installed `@knocklabs/mgmt` and `@knocklabs/node`.
2. Created `app.js` which reads the necessary environment variables (`KNOCK_SERVICE_TOKEN`, `KNOCK_API_TOKEN`, `GMAIL_USER_NAME`, `ZEALT_RUN_ID`).
3. Used the `@knocklabs/mgmt` SDK to upsert a workflow in the `development` environment with `commit: true`. The workflow key is formatted as `top-level-conditions-${run-id}`.
4. Configured the top-level `conditions` group to require `recipient.role` to be `equal_to` `"admin"`.
5. Added a single channel step for `mailtrap` with the specified subject `Admin alert ${run-id}` and an HTML body addressing the recipient by name.
6. Activated the workflow in the `development` environment via the Management API.
7. Triggered the workflow using the `@knocklabs/node` SDK with inline recipient identification for both an `"admin"` and a `"viewer"` user. Both recipients use the plus-addressing convention for the test Gmail inbox.
8. Written the resulting `workflow_run_id` to `/home/user/myproject/output.log` in the exact format required. 

The Knock workflow short-circuits execution for the `"viewer"` recipient, so only the `"admin"` recipient will receive the email in the test inbox.

part.text:
**Confirming Workflow Success**

Everything appears to be in order; the workflow is active, conditions are met, and the trigger was sent as expected. I'm verifying that only the intended recipient, the admin, received it due to the established condition, which aligns with the acceptance criteria.
